summary: Patchwork matrix recording with uneven sound quality but captures a legendary second set – Shakedown, “If I Had the World to Give,” and soaring jams make this show unforgettable.
review: The performance shines brightest in the second set where the band delivers chaotic, space‑filled improvisations highlighted by Shakedown St. and an exquisite rendition of “If I Had the World to Give.”  The audio is a composite of soundboard and audience tapes; while splices and level swings are evident, the mix remains listenable enough to convey the show’s magic.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (≈75 %) award 4–5 stars and praise the musical performance, especially the second‑set improvisations; recording quality is noted as uneven but still usable, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple sources (soundboard + audience) blended with splices; occasional level fluctuations and drop‑outs, but overall clear enough to enjoy the performance.

standout_songs: If I Had the World to Give
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but uneven in levels; second set opens with a free‑form jam and then rolls through a series of unique, spacey improvisations that many reviewers label as ‘must‑listen’ and one‑of‑a‑kind.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice described as “honey on a silver spoon,” especially strong on 1/2 Step, Roses and Peggy-O; Garcia’s passion highlighted throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Slide‑heavy drones praised on Franklin's and ‘If I Had the World to Give’; also noted that he missed most of the second set according to one reviewer.
band_member_comments.Drums: Short drum break early in set two called “amazingly good”; overall percussion supports the free‑form vibe.
